ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for dual-cream filled Asian bakery bun; 'filled bun food product' is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Sweet buns and rolls) is the closest defensible category for a cream-filled sweet bun; no exact code for dual-cream Hokkaido-chocolate variant.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept for this specific bakery product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One piece of Yuan Yang Kazu Kazu bun weighs approximately 80 g, providing about 34 kcal of energy with 8.3 g of carbohydrates and 7.9 g of sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Yuan Yang Kazu Kazu (Hokkaido + Chocolate) — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this product (cr_id G00011025). All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat is not separately reported by HPB for this item; estimated from analogous cream-filled sweet bun data in USDA FoodData Central (USDA SR Legacy, Bread, sweet, enriched, NDB 18002) and documented accordingly.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Yuan Yang Kazu Kazu dual-cream filled bun. GI of 72 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white bread / sweet bun analogues (GI range 70–76 for refined wheat-flour sweet baked goods with added sugar). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (72) × carbohydrate per serving in grams (8.3 g per 80 g piece) / 100 = 6.0. This is a conservative analogous estimate; actual GI may vary depending on cream filling composition.

hypertension.key_factor: High added sugar (9.94 g/100 g) with negligible potassium (5 mg/100 g) and zero dietary fibre; added sugar independently raises blood pressure risk.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this product due to its high added-sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) and near-zero potassium, which together provide no cardiovascular benefit and may independently elevate blood pressure per AHA 2018 guidance on dietary sugar and hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI (~72) with 9.94 g sugar/100 g and zero dietary fibre, resulting in rapid postprandial glucose elevation with no fibre-mediated attenuation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this product; its high glycaemic index (~72), high sugar content (9.94 g/100 g), and complete absence of dietary fibre will cause rapid postprandial blood glucose spikes with no fibre buffer to moderate absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from dual cream fillings (Hokkaido milk and chocolate), combined with high added sugar and zero fibre, contributes to atherogenic dietary pattern.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit this product due to its saturated fat from dual cream fillings and high added-sugar content, both of which contribute to an atherogenic dietary pattern and offer no protective nutrients such as fibre, potassium, or omega-3 fatty acids.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Refined sugar and saturated fat from dual cream fillings are pro-inflammatory; no anti-inflammatory nutrients present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product as its high refined sugar and saturated fat content from Hokkaido milk and chocolate cream fillings promote systemic inflammation, with no compensating anti-inflammatory nutrients such as omega-3, polyphenols, or antioxidants.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yuan Yang Kazu Kazu (Hokkaido Milk and Chocolate Cream Filled Bun) is a soft, sweet Asian bakery bun sold in Singapore, filled with two distinct cream varieties: Hokkaido milk cream and chocolate cream. The name 'Yuan Yang' (鸳鸯) refers to the classic Hong Kong pairing concept of two complementary flavours combined in one item, while 'Kazu Kazu' is a playful brand or product name. The bun is made from enriched wheat flour dough, baked until golden, and injected or filled with both a rich Hokkaido-style milk cream and a chocolate cream filling. The texture is pillowy and soft on the outside with a sweet, creamy, and indulgent interior. Nutritionally, this product is characterised by a very high sugar content (9.94 g/100 g), negligible protein (0 g/100 g confirmed by HPB), low fat (0.25 g/100 g), and zero dietary fibre. Energy density is low at 42 kcal/100 g, reflecting the high water content of the cream fillings. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 72 (high GI), making it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing blood glucose. The product contains allergens including gluten, milk, and eggs. It is classified as ultra-processed and is not recommended for individuals with diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high added-sugar content, saturated fat from cream fillings, and complete absence of protective nutrients such as dietary fibre, potassium, or omega-3 fatty acids. Commonly consumed as a snack or light meal in Singapore's bakery culture.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00011025, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous sweet bun data and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for refined wheat-flour sweet baked goods, clearly documented. All mandatory multilingual names for a Singaporean dish are present.
notes: protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00011025); this is a cream-filled sweet bun where the protein contribution from the small amount of wheat flour and cream is below the HPB reporting threshold — confirmed zero per approved source. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB; refined wheat flour bun with cream filling contains no meaningful fibre. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB; fat content is very low (0.25 g/100 g) and cholesterol is below detection threshold — confirmed zero per approved source. magnesium_mg is reported as 0.0 per HPB; confirmed zero per approved source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific commercial cream-filled bun are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.1 g/100 g from USDA SR Legacy analogous sweet bun data (NDB 18002) given total fat of only 0.25 g/100 g; this is a conservative estimate. Halal status not confirmed from available data; marked false pending vendor certification. Serving size of 80 g is an estimate based on typical Asian bakery bun portion; actual product weight may vary.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (0.0 confirmed zero per HPB), sodium_mg (3.0), saturated_fat_g (0.1 estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogue and documented), sugar_g (9.94) all present as numeric values.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (72), gi_category (high), glycemic_load_per_serving (6.0), gi_source all present. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for refined wheat-flour sweet baked goods, clearly documented with estimation basis.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn filled bun food product (closest_parent match documented), FoodEx2 A0CKP Sweet buns and rolls (closest_parent match documented), SNOMED CT null with gap note. No codes invented; all matches are defensible and documented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is well above the 200-character minimum. Covers food identity, preparation, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.
